stories.title: Quick Actions UX Redesign
acceptance_criteria: Quick Open label above button group
4 buttons in group (Apple pattern)
All buttons work correctly with NSOpenPanel

stories.title: Ask Pixley Opens Browser with Context
acceptance_criteria: Attached file's parent folder set as root
File visible and selected in sidebar
Chat panel opens automatically
Initial question appears in chat
AI response continues in chat panel

stories.title: Ask Pixley File Picker Redesign
acceptance_criteria: Ask Pixley about label with inline file picker button
Standard macOS button style for Choose File
Selected file shown as pill (rounded, clickable to replace)
Pill click replaces file (not separate X button)
Question text field separate from file selection
Remove old doc icon button

stories.title: Fix Drill-Down Bug
stories.root_cause: Security-scoped resource access not maintained for subfolders
acceptance_criteria: Security-scoped resource access maintained for subfolder navigation
Drilling into any folder shows its contents
Works 3+ levels deep
No silent failures - errors logged if access denied
stories.verification: Manual test: Open Documents, drill 3+ levels deep, content shows at each level

stories.title: State Architecture Refactor
acceptance_criteria: AppState split into 4 focused observable objects (NavigationState, DocumentState, ChatState, FolderState)
Each state object available via @Environment
FolderService defined as protocol, injected (not .shared singleton)
All existing functionality works unchanged
Views updated to use specific state objects
stories.verification: Build succeeds, app launches, all v1.0 features work as before

stories.title: Native Sidebar Refactor
acceptance_criteria: Remove custom FolderRowView, FileRowView structs
Use standard List with Label for all rows
Folders use NavigationLink(value:) for push navigation
Files use List(selection:) binding for selection
Markdown count shown via .badge() modifier
Non-markdown files shown but dimmed/disabled
Sort: folders first, then files (alphabetically within groups)
Keyboard navigation works (arrow keys, enter)
VoiceOver announces items correctly
Feels native - portable to iOS unchanged
stories.verification: Sidebar looks and feels like iOS Files app, keyboard navigation works, could copy to iOS with minimal changes

design_decisions.navigation_model: iOS Files app style (push for folders, tap-select for files)
design_decisions.sort_order: Folders first, then files (alphabetically within groups)
